---
description: "Use when editing portfolio landing copy and hero messaging tone/layout."
applyTo: "src/pages/index.astro"
---

# Landing Copy Tone Rules

## Objective

Keep the hero section and top-level messaging informative, concise, and intellectually grounded.

## Copy Hierarchy

- Use a three-layer structure in hero text:
  - Primary statement (largest, concise)
  - Supporting statement (one step smaller)
  - Body copy (smaller, relaxed line-height)
- Keep visual emphasis restrained. Avoid decorative highlighted words unless explicitly requested.

## Tone

- Prefer factual language over promotional slogans.
- Use calm confidence, not aggressive persuasion.
- Emphasize implementation outcomes and domain-structuring ability.
- Keep legal background references present but secondary to engineering substance.

## Typography and Spacing

- Keep typography Mincho-oriented for an intellectual tone.
- Avoid oversized text that fills the viewport and reduces background breathing room.
- Maintain generous spacing between primary heading, supporting statement, and body copy.
- Preserve legibility across breakpoints by reducing size/weight gradually on smaller screens.

## Guardrails

- Do not add visual effects solely for emphasis (badges, strong background chips, heavy gradients on text) unless asked.
- Do not increase copy density in hero to the point where the section appears crowded.
- Keep line length readable (`max-w-*`) and line-height relaxed for body text.
